methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $4.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Nuclear Energy. Range: $1.0B - $10.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Kairos Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Late Stage), post-money $1.6B+

data.narrative:
TerraPower is a late-stage nuclear energy company founded by Bill Gates, currently valued at $4.0B+ on $1.7B+ raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies in the nuclear energy sector: 3 public firms (Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, and others trading under SMR) and 7 private-stage companies, with direct comparables including Kairos Power ($1.6B), X-Energy ($2.0B), and Zap Energy ($1.0B).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for late-stage advancement to the $4.0B last post-money valuation, implying $4.8B. Peer-median analysis across the 5 most comparable firms (Oklo, X-Energy, Kairos Power, Nano Nuclear Energy, Zap Energy) yields a median of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $10.0B. The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity in development stage and public-vs-private status within the nuclear cohort.

The two methods produce a triangulated range of $1.4B to $5.5B, with a median of $3.2B. TerraPower's $4.0B+ current valuation sits in the upper half of this range, supported primarily by stage-progression logic. Key uncertainties include comparability across public and late-stage private peers, and the substantial valuation spread within the nuclear energy sector itself ($1.0B–$10.0B).
